notes: Good session with progress on red tag Tighty Whities. Possible new project Strawberry Scoops.
First session ever on the Midlo board! Hard problems and tricky to adjust to the overall style - not the best try hard but good to mix things up. I either flashed or got shut down with no in between.
Good board session. New project.
Solid session. Good progress on Sirens of Titan.
Back on project Tighty Whities. Completed more moves including pulling over the roof. Good progress and confidence boost.
Another session on Tighty Whities!
Another session on Sirens of Titan with good progress on the last two moves. Still need to link the top two moves.
Changed beta on top sequence of Tighty Whities. Linked from under the roof to the top. Made send attempts and getting close!
Project Tighty Whities sent!
A very high gravity and weak session.
Shopping session for new res tag to project. Possible pockets project, pocket paradise
One good send (V5-) and some potential project candidates
4 hours sleep. All moves worked on Sirens of Titan. Cant do last move.
Good session with progress on Pocket Paradise. Preceded by first crimp ladder training attempt, which limited overall attempts.
Tougher feeling session likely due to new board crimp ladder training added for the first time on Friday
Tough session was not feeling strong or snappy. Transitioned from board to boulders halfway through.
Went into this session with not great sleep, maybe 4-5 hours, but put in decent send attempts on Pocket Paradise.
Okay session with some attempts on Heel Deal and a send of a roof problem on the prow.
Solid board session checking out Theres my Chippy and completing all moves.
Really happy with this session. Went in after a week off. Initial attempts on Pocket Paradise felt off and not close. Changed my beta then linked to the top. Got the send after that!
Session got off to rough start but settled into a lot of quality attempts on The Heel Deal.
This felt like a tougher session but got in attempts on several moderate climbs including a V4 (MD, left side of prow), another V4 (Ken, right side of prow), and got a send on a V5 prow problem by TC
A great session with multiple flashes of new boulders, Greatest Hits and Fly Like The Wind. Also sent Theres my Chippy, which was my second session on this one.

First session since Tuesday board session and after being sick for several days. Overall good! Sent one orange tag (V4), worked on another orange tag with a tricky dead point over a roof, worked on a couple of moves on a red tag, and easily sent two yellow tag climbs in the V2 range.
Overall solid sessions with quality attempts on The Heel Deal and what felt like real, tangible progress.

A decent session for having been rather sick all week. Knocked out some easy boulders. Put in a few attempts on a V6 (probably more like a V3) involving a big start move. Completed the other moves. Did all the moves on another Augie V3+.

A solid session after shifting gears from the board, which wasn't going well, to set boulders. Climbed an Augie V4+ after working all the moves. Did all but the crux move on a more stout feeling V3+ in the same area.

Still struggling to feel rested and strong from being sick. Set a goal of doing 3 easy board climbs. Sent Lefty Loosey, Greatest Hits and (not quite) Huck Tuah (couldn't stick final move). They all felt hard but glad I sent an achievable goal.
An odd session balancing some climbing and working at the gym. Started exploring an Augie V5+ on the right side of the prow (Orange Julius). A possible project.
A challenging session knocking out an off-style V4- dihedral climb and with lots of attempts hitting every move on Orange Julius.

Another struggle session on the board, although it was better than my last session. Still struggled on the last move on Huck Tuah (typically a warm up climb). Put some repeat attempts in on Space Cadet. Started developing a new problem that I think will be interesting.

An okay session mostly trying climbs set by Augie. Got stumped on an Augie V3+ but completed all but the intro moves after a good 4-5 attempts. Completed more than half the moves on another Augie V5. Attempt moves on a V4+ set by TC.

A decent session getting more volume in with the board set at 23-24 degrees (set that way when I got there). I repeated multiple climbs including Lefty Loosey, Huck Tuah, Ibex, Space Cadet, and climbed Sirens of Titan after a few attempts (and adding in a more ergonomic left foot). Getting movement more dialed in for Sirens of Titan felt good and I can return to that at the 30 degree project angle.

First time on Peak Boulders in nearly a month! Good finger training after warm up. Flashed an orange tag then started working the moves on a steep red tag climb with moves going under the roof. Worked all the major moves - roof feels like the crux. Ended the session with a flash of another orange tag. Pretty good session overall!

Good session overall focusing on boulders set by Augie. A V3+ in the far left dihedral continues to put me in weird boxy positions. A new V5+ (Bluefin) on the prow roof seems pretty hard and interesting. A possible project.
[Notes corrupted - original text lost]

A fun and fairly chill social session. Put a few more attempts in on the roof red tag problem. Still need to sort out my toe hook beta. Made most of the moves on an orange tag in the same front roof area. This one is harder than I expected it to be!
A fairly chill session. Climbed a V4 long haul problem on the prow on my second attempt. Pretty pumpy! Climbed several other easy things.
[Notes corrupted - original text lost]
Decent session with some warm ups on the 45 and took more stabs at the red tag roof climb.
A pretty low energy session. Took a few attempts to send a V4 set by MD. Was not able to repeat the first few moves of another V4 set by Augie but did complete the top half of the climb.
Overall a very poor board session. Spent some attempts on Sirens of Titan but struggled to make any moves. Likely all due to very limited sleep; just had trouble sleeping!
Good session knocking out two orange tags relatively quickly. Spent more time on the board doing wall crawls for the first half of the session.

Overall a good session. Climbed a new orange tag (V4-V6) on my second attempt. So probably  soft V4. I tried three other orange tag climbs, one (Yellow holds with a low start) felt very doable. Two other climbs, particularly one with the white dual tex holds, felt much harder. These should make good second tier climbs. I put in a few attempts on a red tag (V6-V8) involving various blue edges and sloper rails. Seems hard!

First session working again with Coach Matt. 

Today was the first session with the board set at 35 degrees. This is the maximum angle for this board. 

My goal was to focus on doing/completing moves rather than trying to send climbs (I didn't send anything). Overall I felt pretty good and this will be the new default angle. I feel good about making this the standard at the beginning of the year. 

Greatest hits. First climb I got on. Not bad intro moves and then focused on sticking the bigger left hand drive-by move to the jug. I tried some different foot beta but felt better/closer with my original method. Seems doable next session. 

Fly like the wind. Somewhat awkward start to the left hand baseball hold. I was able to link to the top starting from a couple moves in. It took me several more tries to stick the first move. After that I just need to bump my right hand to the flat pinch then up to the holds I linked from. 

Ibex. This was my project at 30 degrees this time last year. Starting moves on crimps felt tough but doable. The undercling felt pretty horrible. And the next in-cut crimp felt pretty hard. Will have to go back and see how different beta options feel. 

New set. I started drafting a new problem that starts with the undercling pinches in the middle of the board. I sort of like how the first few moves were coming together. Not sure where I want to take it from there - I have several things in draft I also need to go back to and publish. 

For the next session I feel like I still want to go back and check out more problems at this angle -  we've got decent library of 40-50 climbs at this point (along with some others that are just bad haha).
Flashed one orange tag boulder after training on the Peak Board. Attempted one additional orange tag but got stuck at the crux.

This felt like a more challenging session. I worked on all but the last mantle move of an orange tag and then explored 3 red tag climbs. I think the red tag on the slightly over hanging arete felt the best as I was able to access all of the holds easily.

A solid session overall, this being the second session with the board set at 35 degrees. A send of ÃƒÂ¢Ã‚Â€Ã‚Âœgreatest hitsÃƒÂ¢Ã‚Â€Ã‚Â and started putting together a new problem, ÃƒÂ¢Ã‚Â€Ã‚Âœgold rush.ÃƒÂ¢Ã‚Â€Ã‚Â

Rough. Overall a â€œmehâ€ feeling session. I got on several red tags but didnâ€™t feel particularly hyped or like I was moving well on any of them. I felt the best at the end of the session on a steeper orange tag with incut crimps.
Todayâ€™s session was good! A send of â€œBreakfast in The Shire.â€ And then continued work on Butterball, Gold Rush, and Bear Hug. I think I want to dig in more on Bear Hug.

Overall a productive session after being in a funk on Peak boulders for 2-3 weeks. Put down several climbs set by Augie in the 2-3 range. Worked on a V4. And started projecting an Augie V6, which Iâ€™ll call Jigglypuff.
Good session. One new send of â€œjugzforlife.â€ Attempted a repeat of â€œbreakfast in the shire.â€ Started working more on â€œBear hugâ€ with marginal progress on the first move.

Worked through more Augie boulders including a flash of a powerful V3 on the prow. Continued to put in attempts on a V4 and made marginal progress. Attempted different beta on the V6 project but tweaked my left knee. 

It turns out my knee injury is a minor meniscus tear.
Working through my left knee tweak. It did another big mechanical pop on my warm up. Started setting a low traverse on the board called â€œMe Peg Leg.â€ Need to let my knee heal up.

First session getting back on the wall after tearing my left meniscus. Took it very easy on easy climbs. Climbing on the board felt too spicy but overall was very happy with progress and recovery so far!
More volume on easy problems testing out the knee. Mostly okay. However in the evening my knee really locked up which didnâ€™t feel good. Probably need to dial things back.
Stuck to easy warm-up level boulders today working on applying more tension in my left leg.
Managed to string together a few moves on the board. Got a little more tension out of my left leg.
Made some moves on a harder red tag climb on the 45 wall and felt like I was able to get some more tension in my left leg. Good progress!
Still working through left knee recovery but managed to put some good movement in on orange tags on the 45 degree wall.

A good and fun board session. I was able to tap into a little bit of try hard and got some glimmers of feeling like myself again. 

I stuck to the exact same climbs as last week, managing to send â€œramen leftâ€ but understandably struggling a little bit on the opposite side. I did manage to do all the moves on the mirrored version though. 

I took some stabs at some other harder moves that I tried last week and also felt progress on these moves.
Progress working through moves on the 45 degree wall. Left knee is still vulnerable feeling in higher flexion ranges of motion. Managed to send one easy orange tag and work moves on a red tag.

I opted for the board today at 30 degrees since I only had just over an hour today for my session. My plan was to see if I could get some repeats and sends of climbs I was trying at 35. 

Ramen left, flash 
Ramen right, flash and a repeat send with different (more left foot focused) beta - this is the mirrored climbed I wasnâ€™t able to fully piece together last board session 

Space cadet. A left to right traverse that took me several tries of re-working the moves. 

Purple jolly rancher. I was trying this climb last session at 35. I gave it two attempts from the start and then focused on the top since my go-go gadget arms method wasnâ€™t working. After sorting that out I did manage to send the climb.
A pretty rough session overall. But I have to take into consideration the pretty good Sunday board session.
First session since knee injury where I felt like my movement was more natural. I was able to engage and flex my left knee more and able to tap into a little more try hard. Progress!
A decent board session with a repeat of Jugz4Days and Breakfast in the Shire.

An okay session with some continued work on a red tag climb on the 45 degree wall. While I felt okay I donâ€™t think Iâ€™m going to be making much more progress on this one so Iâ€™ll likely throw in the towel. I think shifting focus to less intense climbs as I continue to rehab my knee might be the better move. Still this was a good climb to re-engage on trying hard.

A decent session. Aimed to send Huck Tuah at 35 but couldnâ€™t pull it all together so I want to return to it next session. I also want to put more effort into Me Peg Leg. Need to continue resending climbs at 35.

One orange tag flash and attempts on two other orange tags. 

Large black holds. First climb where I tested more left heel hooking abilities. It felt decent and I was able to climb through it. Need to sort out the top crux crimp moves. 

Purple holds. 2nd session on this climb and itâ€™s hard for me. Need to sort out the sloper moves and top section. 

Pink holds. Seems doable. Will come back to this.
For this short session I focused on the pink holds climb on the wave feature. I completed all but the crux move and this felt like good progress.

A decent session. Sent Earl Grey Cold at 35. Tried to send Ramen Right but still struggled to get good tension on the outside edge of my left foot. That being said I felt closer to sticking the move. 

I put in attempts on Huck Tuah but still need to stick the top move and link from the start more efficiently.

Possibly the first session where I felt more natural in my movement and my knee was less of an inhibitor. I sent an orange tag climb and put in some additional effort on other orange tags. I need to return to the pink holds climb on the Wave. And still need to tackle the purple holds climb.

First time on triangle boulders in a while. Hopped around on a V5 (blue ripple holds), an Augie V4 (I did most of the moves) and a much harder V5 with a frankly frustrating low sit start. Overall a decent session!

Overall a productive session! Trying to build up a little more volume and capacity, I started with repeats of â€œRamen Left,â€ and â€œJugz4Days.â€ I also finally sent â€œRamen Right.â€ I put some effort into the final crux move of â€œHuck Tuah.â€ Still finding it challenging to maintain tension on my left leg but overall the effort was good. Last, I put in some attempts on â€œSki Ball.â€ I need to complete and link the last few moves on this one.

Overall a good session. I sent (or re-sent?) an orange tag. From there I spent most of my time on the harder orange tag with purple holds. I did the top couple of moves but couldnâ€™t quite link from the start through the middle crux. Still todo: 

Purple holds 
Yellow dual tex holds 
Blue holds on the 45 
Pink holds on the wave boulder 
Front area blue holds roof

A good session overall. I still was not able to stick the final move on Huck Tuah so this needs to be a focus. I was able to hit all but the final two moves on Ski Ball so this is another one to work on and wrap up.

Possibly the first session where I felt more like myself. My plan is to focus most of my climbing on the new set in the front area. I flashed an easy orange tag. Then I did most of the moves on another orange tag (low start with black pockets). I was able to link from the sloper move to the top. Next session I need to link from the start to the sloper and shop around for a red tag project.

A great feeling session. I flashed one V3 Boulder set by Augie. I climbed another Augie V3 on my second attempt. I flashed a V3 set by MD. Then I spent some time on the Prow on a V4+ set by TC. I did all of the moves and made two big overlapping links. Wasnâ€™t able to send at the end of the session but should be able to tackle it next session.

I went into this session feeling rather sick. Instead of projecting I decided to see a new, relatively easy problem called Cabbage Patch. I made all of the moves then climbed it. After that I worked on some normal set boulders, spotting a couple of good potential projects on the prow.
Mostly focused on moderate and harder boulders. A challenging V3 set by Augie and a V5 on the prow. Think I want to project the V5 and will call it Sawmill.

Overall an okay session with most of it spent trying the last move on Huck Tuah. Itâ€™s still giving me trouble but I did manage to try different beta options. Back stepping my right foot might work. I also put some attempts in on the mirrored version of Purple Jolly Rancher. For the next session I think I want to table Huck Tuah, put in send attempts on Ski Ball, and put effort in on Purple Jolly Rancher.

Solid session working on the prow project Sawmill. I focused mainly on the crux moves toward the end. Still need to figure out this beta and put some more commitment in my left leg. Made some other good links from the start and middle moves. Completed the top moves. Next session: continue working crux and link from the start to the crux.
Overall a good session focusing almost exclusively on the crux moves of Sawmill. Still havenâ€™t quite figured out my beta though.

The board today was set at 33 degrees which gave me more room to actually use the crimps. Today I worked on: 

Carthage delenda est
They Live
Verde (didnâ€™t like this one but want to try the first move again)

Great session with multiple sends including my first Red Tag (V6-8) send of the year. Granted, it was pretty soft but Iâ€™ll take it. 

Sends: 
- Orange tag, 2nd attempt. A crimpy style 
- Orange tag, flash. Easier. 
- Red tag, 3rd attempt. 
- Orange tag, 2nd session. A good win on this one to solve the final move and put it all together.

During my warmup my knee locked up. This was the first lock up in about 12 weeks which was quite frustrating. Fortunately there was no pain and I managed to keep moving on. I spent the first half of the session doing some practice on easier climbs set by Augie. 

From there I shifted to the Sawmill project. I had a good rip from the start to the crux. From there I worked the crux a few more times, managing to actually hold a good left heel hook going into the crux move. Progress.

A decent session at 30 degrees mostly focused on working the smaller crimp moves on Me Peg Leg. I want to complete this one in my next weekend session. I also put a couple goes on Carthago Delenda Est.
Good session overall putting in a few attempts on a hard crimpy red tag, and getting a send on an orange tag after about 4 or 5 attempts.
A decent session continuing to work through the crux beta on sawmill. Possibly a breakthrough using a heel hook. Also put some effort in another V5 on the side of the prow.

A decent session sending â€œme peg legâ€ at 33 degrees. I also made the top move of â€œhuck tuahâ€ but was unable to link the entire climb from the start. Overall not bad and the first session in a while where I felt like I was trying harder.
A great session with maximum effort on Sawmill. Committed to the heel hook to stick the crux move. Still need to link to the top.
Project Sawmill COMPLETE
Good session at 33 degrees today. Climbed Uncanny Valley after a few tries. Fun beta!
Another red tag send. Second attempt which leads me to believe it is quite soft but thatâ€™s okay. Second red tag send so far this year!
My knee locked up again warming up at home in a deep squat on my toes. I still stuck to my board session, just focusing on moving between crimps at 30 degrees. Overall movement felt awful though.
Overall a good session. Completed two new orange tag climbs. Climbed the first half of a crimpy red tag. Climbed the first half of another red tag.

A good session to repeat the orange tag arete climb. Twice repeated the crimpy orange tag climb. Climbed a new orange tag on the 45 degree wall. I chose not to attempt any red tags today given limited sleep and early stressful wake up. 

For next session I need to focus in on the two crimpy orange tag climbs and put more effort into the red tags on the new set.

An okay session focusing primarily on trying harder and crimp focused res tag moves. 

Red tag on the 45. Need to work all the moves. 
Crimp red tag. Still hard and need to work moves. 
Red tag dihedral. Did all the moves. Need to do top move.

Not the best session despite making marginal progress on a couple of yellow tags. Some over indulgence the previous day left me feeling very unable to try hard. Time to improve diet and cut back on drinking. Itâ€™s impacting my performance.
A productive session and went into this one feeling much more rested. I completed a V6 set by Ken and fully linked (minus one move) a V5+ set by Augie. This one felt particularly anti-style.

Good session! Climbed a harder orange tag on my second attempt. Put in other attempts on several new tags. For my next session I have multiple orange tag climbs on both the current and new set on the wave boulder that I need to put down.
This session started on the board then transitioned to boulders. Felt good on a couple of Augie V5s! Almost have one wrapped up. Next session wrap up the blue holds Augie V5.

A really solid session starting on the midlo board. Made good progress on two climbs: The Worlds Smallest Meghan, and DK2. After that I moved to regular boulders where I sent and worked several orange tag climbs.
Generally good session on new boulders. Mostly focused on orange tags. Climbed a fairly challenging orange tag on my 2nd attempt involving some crimps. Others I need to return too.

A good session overall mostly focused on specific harder movements involving my left leg. I returned to Ibex and need to work on the technique for the left hand undercling. 

Two new problems by Scott are both good and worth returning to.

Good session with a send of a new orange tag. A send on the coordination orange tag boulder (second session) and good effort on a crimpy red tag. For next session I need to focus on the second half of this boulder and also get in good link attempts through the middle section. Need to work on foot beta options going into the middle section.

A good session on the midlo board with a send of “the world’s smallest meaghan” after trying it the previous week. Good takeaway in terms of crimping, getting my thumb wrapped around the hold, and straitening my arm under the hold.
Mostly focused on moving from and to the more challenging holds at 30 degrees. The large and smaller flat holds. Using the mirror helped here. I think this will be a good exercise to return to.

A pretty rough session overall and had trouble finding the right gear. I ultimately sent a new orange tag and nearly flashed a steep and powerful orange tag in the front area. No luck on the crimpy project red tag.

A very solid and efficient training session on the board. I again focused only on doing a few hard crimp moves rather than working full problems. I completed these moves on both sides of the board. After about 40 minutes I shifted to normal Triangle boulders (see associated Climbs for this session).

Good session overall. I need to return to and complete the new orange tag climb with blue “brain” holds as this one proved rather tricky. Repeating the new yellow tag crimp climb would also be good. Most importantly I put good effort in on what I’m now calling Crimp Gimp, a red tag project.
